topic: Auto-expiry alerts for work permits and visas — how the dashboard surfaces renewals 30/60/90 days out
format: Compare 'how most Oman businesses do X today' with 'how SmartPro Hub customers do X'
post:
Most Oman businesses track visa expiries in a spreadsheet someone last updated three months ago.

SmartPro Hub customers get a live dashboard that flags every expiry 90, 60, and 30 days out — automatically.

That difference is worth understanding.

One lapsed work permit can trigger Ministry of Labour penalties, blow up a project mid-delivery, and cost more in emergency renewal fees than a full year's subscription to any HR platform. That's a bad day that's entirely avoidable.

But the default fix is still a colour-coded Excel sheet that belongs to one PRO officer — who happens to be on annual leave when the alert was needed.

We built the expiry alert system because the real risk isn't forgetting a date. It's not knowing which dates exist in the first place.

The dashboard pulls every employee's permit and visa data into one view. Three alert windows: 90 days (plan), 60 days (initiate), 30 days (escalate). No manual chasing. No buried email threads.

Customers across Oman and the GCC use it to run renewal cycles for 50–300 employees the same way they'd manage a sales pipeline — with visibility, clear stages, and no last-minute scrambling.

Work permit tracking GCC teams can actually trust.

How many active permits does your team track right now — and do you know exactly when the next one expires?

Book a 20-minute demo: https://www.thesmartpro.io/demo?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=general
